sections.paragraph:
δωδεκαετής, ες pert. to a period of time lasting twelve years, twelve years (1 Esdr 5:41; Jos., Ant. 15, 9, 6 [341] ‘within twelve years’; Plut.) twelve years old γενομένης δὲ δωδεκαετοῦς (for ιβ´ ἐτοῦς pap) when she (Mary) was twelve years old GJs 8:2 (As read by Tdf., s. his and deStrycker’s app.)—DELG s.v. δώδεκα.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 266
